scenario: Nordic Logistics ships 4200 orders per day and is standardising its driver acceptance task across all branches on Camunda 8.8 Forms. A new forms developer is preparing a checklist of the element categories explicitly documented in the quickstart so the team only uses palette items the docs name, and the team lead wants the checklist ready within 1 hour.
question: Which set of element categories does the Camunda 8.8 forms quickstart explicitly document as available in the palette?

options.text: Text fields, numerical values, checkboxes, radio elements, BPMN tasks, and signal events that can be embedded into the form palette
Text fields, password fields, file upload widgets, signature pads, audio recorders, and barcode scanners listed inside the form palette
Text fields, numerical values, checkboxes, radio elements, selection menus, text components, and buttons listed inside the form palette
Text fields, numerical values, image carousels, video players, chart widgets, and embedded maps available inside the form palette

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN tasks and signal events are diagram constructs, not form elements. The quickstart enumerates only form element categories such as text fields, numerical values, checkboxes, radio elements, selection menus, text components, and buttons.

b.text: Incorrect. The quickstart does not list password fields, signature pads, audio recorders, or barcode scanners. Those are not part of the documented set of palette categories for Camunda 8.8 Forms.

c.text: Correct. The quickstart names exactly this set of element categories available in Forms: text fields, numerical values, checkboxes, radio elements, selection menus, text components, and buttons. The element library reference covers each in detail.

d.text: Incorrect. Image carousels, video players, chart widgets, and embedded maps are not documented palette categories. The quickstart's catalogue is centred on form input controls and basic content components.

explanation: The quickstart in the utilizing-forms page enumerates the palette categories available to the form designer: text fields, numerical values, checkboxes, radio elements, selection menus, text components, and buttons. The Camunda Forms reference is where each of these categories is described in detail.
